item.title: Women's elite sports revenues to reach $3 billion in 2026, Deloitte says
item.description: Global revenues in women's elite sports will reach at least $3 billion for the first time вҖӢin 2026, marking an increase of 25% вҖҢcompared to last year, Deloitte said in a report on Wednesday.

item.title: Russia sees China's WeChat, Douyin as models for its homegrown Max messenger
item.description: Russia sees Chinese tech giant Tencent's WeChat messaging app and TikTok sister app Douyin as models for developing its own struggling вҖӢMax messaging app, Vladimir Kiriyenko, CEO of state-controlled internet firm VK , вҖҢsaid on Wednesday.

item.title: US mortgage rates edge down to 6.51%, MBA says
item.description: The interest rate on the most popular U.S. home loan ticked down last week for the first вҖӢtime since the start of the Iran war, but вҖҢnot enough to rejuvenate a housing market that's pricing out many buyers with a combination of elevated borrowing costs and expensive вҖӢhomes.

item.title: Levi shares rise as pricey denim sales smooth over tariff hit
item.description: Levi Strauss' shares jumped about 12% on Wednesday as investors cheered its strongest quarterly revenue growth in nearly four years with confidence growing in the denim maker's вҖӢability to stave off tariff hits with more full-price sales.

item.title: Exxon signals Q1 upstream profit bump from Iran war, downstream boost in later quarters
item.description: Higher oil and gas вҖҢprices due to the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran could boost Exxon Mobil's first-quarter upstream earnings by up to $2.9 billion, outweighing the impact of disruptions to some of its oil and gas production in the Middle East, the U.S. oil producer said on Wednesday.

item.title: Morning Bid: Big relief rally, for now
item.description: A look at the day ahead in U.S. and global markets from Alun John

item.title: Ackman's UMG bid sees analysts split on BollorГ©'s next move
item.description: Market analysts are split over whether Universal Music Group's top stakeholders will support billionaire Bill Ackman's Pershing Square proposed $64 billion takeover of the music label.

item.title: Delta pulls growth plans as fuel spike drives up costs, dents Q2 profit
item.description: Delta Air Lines on Wednesday forecast lower-than-expected profit for вҖӢthe second quarter and said it was too early to update its full-year outlook, citing uncertainty caused by a run-up in jet fuel prices вҖҢlinked to the Iran war.

item.title: Grab to lean on scale, AI to navigate rising fuel costs, CEO says
item.description: Southeast Asia's top ride-hailing and delivery firm Grab believes artificial intelligence-led products вҖҢand services will help it drive growth and navigate challenges such as affordability and rising fuel costs in the wake of the war in Iran, company CEO Anthony Tan told Reuters.

item.title: Why the bond market won't bounce back to pre-war levels
item.description: Global bond markets may rebound but are unlikely to fully recover from the war-driven selloff because, even if there is peace, energy prices and inflation will run hotter for longer.

item.title: Fed minutes of March meeting could flesh out how policymakers view war risks to economy
item.description: U.S. Federal Reserve officials knew at their March meeting that the U.S.-Iran war was going to send inflation higher for вҖӢthe year, but minutes of the two-day session to be released Wednesday afternoon may flesh out even further the risks policymakers and central вҖҢbank staff see from a conflict that President Donald Trump has cast in civilizational terms.

item.title: US bank profits to rise on deals, but Iran war fuels outlook uncertainty
item.description: Large U.S. banks are set to post higher quarterly earnings on strong interest income and investment banking fees, analyst estimates show, though investors will focus on вҖӢforecasts as rising geopolitical risks involving Iran add to macroeconomic uncertainty.

item.title: Shah Capital intends to vote against Novavax's board nominees, executive compensation
item.description: Activist investor Shah Capital plans to vote against the re-election of board nominees and the executive вҖӢcompensation package at Novavax's upcoming annual meeting, renewing pressure for вҖҢchanges at the vaccine maker.

item.title: Volatility-linked funds put March US stock selling spree in the rearview mirror
item.description: The heaviest stock selling by volatility-linked funds appears to have largely run its course last вҖҢmonth, potentially setting up U.S. indexes for gains should market swings subside in coming weeks.

item.title: Tecto plans new Brazil data centers, part of $2 billion investment
item.description: Tecto Data Centers, backed by BTG Pactual's funds, is set to invest $2 billion through 2028, вҖӢincluding the construction of five data centers in Brazil, the вҖҢcompany told Reuters, adding to a wave of new data centers in Latin America's No. 1 economy.

item.title: Swatch urges shareholders to vote against activist investor's bid for board seat
item.description: Swatch has once again opposed GreenWood Investors' bid for a seat on the board and has urged shareholders to vote against вҖӢthe activist investor at its upcoming annual general meeting, the вҖҢwatchmaker said on Wednesday.

item.title: Samsung Electronics' shareholder Shinhan Bank launches $2.1 billion share sale - term sheet
item.description: Samsung Electronics shareholder Shinhan вҖҢBank launched a sale of about $2.1 billion worth of shares in the South вҖӢKorean company, according to a вҖӢterm sheet seen by Reuters on вҒ Wednesday.

item.title: How the math works on a $1.75 trillion SpaceX valuation
item.description: Elon MuskвҖҷs SpaceX is seeking a $1.75 trillion valuation in its forthcoming initial public offering. How far into the stratosphere is that?

item.title: Global banks scale back China rate-cut calls, see policy rate on hold this year
item.description: Major global investment banks now expect China to keep official interest rates steady this year, scaling back earlier rate-cut calls, as the impact from the Middle East conflict appears вҖӢlimited, even as Beijing maintains a loose policy stance.

item.title: Greece to ban social media for under-15s from 2027
item.description: Greece will ban access вҖӢto social media for children under the age вҖҢof 15 from January 1, 2027, Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis said on Wednesday, citing rising anxiety, sleep problems and the вҖӢaddictive design of online platforms.

item.title: Wall Street futures climb on relief from US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: U.S. stock index futures climbed on Wednesday вҖӢas investors breathed a sigh of relief after the U.S. and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire, sending вҖҢcrude prices lower on expectations that energy supplies through the Strait of Hormuz could resume.

item.title: General Motors recalls over 270,000 cars in US over rearview camera issue
item.description: General Motors is recalling 271,770 вҖӢcars in the вҖҢU.S. over an issue with the вҖӢrearview camera, вҖӢthe U.S. National Highway вҒ Traffic Safety вҖӢAdministration (NHTSA) said on Wednesday.

item.title: Stellantis in advanced talks to develop Opel EV with China's Leapmotor, sources say
item.description: Stellantis is in advanced talks with Leapmotor to jointly develop an Opel-branded electric SUV that вҖӢwould use the Chinese automaker's technology and be produced at Stellantis' Zaragoza plant in Spain, three sources told Reuters.

item.title: Greece expected to announce social media ban for children under 15
item.description: Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos вҖӢMitsotakis is expected вҖҢon Wednesday to announce вҖӢa ban вҖӢon access to social вҒ media for вҖӢchildren under the вҖӢage of 15, according to a вҖӢgovernment briefing.

item.title: Swiss banks to test use cases for Swiss franc stablecoin
item.description: Six Swiss banks have joined forces to вҖӢtest potential use cases вҖҢfor a Swiss franc stablecoin in Switzerland, banking giant UBS вҖӢsaid on Wednesday.

item.title: Shell flags lower gas output, capital outflow amid Iran conflict but sees oil trading boost
item.description: Shell said on Wednesday that weaker first-quarter вҖҢgas output and a hit to short-term liquidity would be offset partly by stronger oil trading, offering an early glimpse into how the U.S.-Israeli war on Iranis reshaping oil majors' earnings.

item.title: SK Hynix shares jump 15% after peer Samsung projects blowout earnings
item.description: Shares of South Korean вҖӢchipmaker SK Hynix surged on Wednesday вҖҢafter peer Samsung Electronics forecast market-beating quarterly earnings, fuelling expectations about SK Hynix's results.

item.title: China's Xi urges demand-driven growth in services industry
item.description: China's President Xi Jinping вҖӢhas called for вҖҢa demand-driven approach coupled with reform and вҖӢtechnological empowerment вҖӢto develop the service вҒ industry, the official вҖӢXinhua news agency вҖӢreported on Wednesday.

item.title: Morning Bid: Two weeks to breathe
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Ankur Banerjee

item.title: US carmakers accuse EU of blocking supersized pick-up trucks from roads, FT reports
item.description: U.S. carmakers have accused Brussels вҖӢof keeping their largest вҖҢpick-up trucks, including the Ford F-150, вҖӢthe Chevy Silverado вҖӢand the Ram 1500, вҒ off European roads, вҖӢthe Financial Times вҖӢreported on Wednesday.
